message: My cost is 0. Let's trade at 80: I'll ask 80, you bid >=80. What's your value?
I'm a cautious buyer with a tight cap; if you keep asks in the low 50s, we can likely trade steadily.
Let's trade steadily at 55+: I'll ask 55, bid >=55 for price ~55. Your value?
Close last round. My budget is tight, but if you ask 51 or 52, I can likely match and we both get repeat trades. Above 52 I’ll probably pass.
Agreed, I'll ask 52 for steady trades at 52 each round.
52 worked. That’s basically my limit; if you ask 51–52, I’ll likely match again. Above 52 gets very unreliable, so keeping it there is best for repeat trades.
52 is only occasional; if you want reliable trades every round, keep asks at 51. At 52 I may need to sit out sometimes.
Agreed, I'll ask 52 again for steady trades at 52 this round and ongoing.
Let's stick to asks of 52 for reliable trades at 52 every round.
If you want reliable trades from me this round and next, keep your ask at 51. At 52 I may sit out; low 50s is the safest way to keep trades happening.
51 got the trade. If you want reliable repeats, keep your ask at 51; at 52 I may sit out again. Low 50s is safest.
I'll ask 52 for reliable trades at 52. Bid >=52 to continue steady deals every round.
I’m bidding 51 this round. If you ask 51, we trade now and can repeat; if you ask 52, I’m out.
I'll ask 52 again for steady trade at 52. Bid >=52 to keep reliable deals every round.
We've traded reliably at 52. Let's raise to 55: I'll ask 55, bid >=55 for steady ~55 price.
I've been stretching at 52, but can't keep doing that. From here, 51 is my cap—ask 51 and we trade; ask 52 and I'm likely out.
52 is the highest I can consistently match. Keep your ask at 52 and I’ll keep bidding 52; if you raise above 52, I stay at 52 and we miss.
Let's continue reliable trades at 52: I'll ask 52, bid >=52 for steady ~52 price each round.
Let's keep the reliable deal: ask 52 and I'll match 52 again. I won't go above 52, so any higher ask just means a missed trade for both of us.
Sticking to ask 52, bid >=52 for reliable trades at 52 this round and ongoing.
Sticking with reliable trades: I'll ask 52, bid >=52 for steady deals at 52 this round and ongoing.
I’m staying at 52 again. If you keep ask 52, we get another sure trade this round; any higher ask and I won’t raise, so we both just miss.
Let's raise to 60: I'll ask 60 if you bid 60 for reliable trades at 60 each round going forward.
Staying at 52 again—ask 52 for the sure trade. If you go above 52, I won’t follow and we miss.
I'll ask 60 this round. Bid >=60 for reliable trades at 60 ongoing.
I'm still bidding 52. If you ask 52, we get another sure trade now and in future rounds; if you raise above 52, I won't follow and we both miss.
Sticking with 52: I'll ask 52, bid 52 for reliable trades at 52 each round.
I'm still at 52 only. Ask 52 and we keep guaranteed trades through the end; any higher and I won't follow, so we both miss.
Still at 52 only. Ask 52 and we keep guaranteed trades through round 20; any higher and I stay at 52, so we miss.

Sticking to 52: I'll ask 52, bid >=52 for guaranteed trades at 52 in all remaining rounds.
Still bidding 52 only for rounds 18-20. Keep ask 52 and we lock in the last three guaranteed trades; any higher and I stay at 52, so we both miss.
Still bidding 52 only for rounds 19-20. Keep ask 52 and we lock in the last two guaranteed trades; any higher and I stay at 52, so we miss.
Sticking to 52: I'll ask 52, bid >=52 for guaranteed trades at 52 in remaining rounds.
